title: Simple DNA extraction for museum beetle specimens to unlock genetic data from historical collections
author: Al-Dhafer, Balaji, et al.

steps.title: Tissue preparation and disruption
steps.instruction: 1.Excise tissue samples from the selected beetle parts, about 5–10 mg, using sterile scalpels and tweezers.2.Transfer tissues into sterile microcentrifuge tubes.3.Rapid freeze the tissues in liquid nitrogen.4.Conduct mechanical rupture of tissues using a sterile mortar and pestle.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of chloroform to the lysates
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of chloroform to the lysates.

steps.title: Gently invert the tubes for mixing for about 10 mins
steps.instruction: Gently invert the tubes for mixing for about 10 mins.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 12,000 rpm for 15 mins at room temperature
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 12,000 rpm for 15 mins at room temperature.

steps.title: Carefully transfer the aqueous phase containing...
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer the aqueous phase containing DNA into a new microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Spectrophotometric analysis
steps.instruction: Spectrophotometric analysis revealed A260/A280 ratios between 1.7 and 1.9.

steps.title: Spectrophotometric analysis
steps.instruction: This suggests that the DNA obtained had high purity, despite the age and conditions of the samples.

steps.title: PCR product analysis
steps.instruction: PCR products were checked by electrophoresis on a 1.2 % agarose gel in 0.5x TBE buffer.

steps.title: PCR product analysis
steps.instruction: Successful amplification of the COI (650 bp) barcode marker was achieved.

steps.title: PCR product analysis
steps.instruction: Well-defined bands, corresponding to the expected amplicon sizes, were observed (Fig. 3B).

steps.title: PCR product analysis
steps.instruction: In well-preserved specimens, higher molecular weight bands confirmed that the protocol can recover intact DNA under good preservation conditions.

description: The improved CTAB protocol was validated based on the quality and yield of DNA extracted from beetle specimens that were aged for over ∼45 years.
